task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.
In the OpenBCI GUI setup screen, 'SYNTHETIC' is selected as the data source (often under 'Synthetic' or 'Simulate' options) before starting the system.

task_completion.description: The EEG session is started and data is streaming
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenBCI GUI dashboard is active, showing live scrolling EEG data in the Time Series widget.

task_completion.description: At least 10 seconds of data is recorded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The recording timer in the OpenBCI GUI shows 00:00:10 or greater, OR a saved file is shown with a size of at least ~167 KB (which corresponds to 10 seconds of 250Hz 8-channel data).

task_completion.description: The recording is saved to the default location
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer or terminal shows a newly created OpenBCI recording file (typically named OpenBCI-RAW-...) located in the ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/ directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI to generate the recording
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the OpenBCI GUI interface rather than just writing a script or text file.

integrity.description: The saved file appears to be a genuine OpenBCI output
integrity.visual_evidence: The file name in the Recordings directory follows the standard OpenBCI auto-generated naming convention (e.g., OpenBCI-RAW-YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS.txt) and is not a manually named dummy file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to fake the file creation
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history does not show commands like 'touch', 'echo', or 'dd' being used to create a fake file of the target size in the Recordings directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI GUI default recording directory is /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings. The SYNTHETIC data source simulates an 8-channel Cyton board at a 250 Hz sample rate. A genuine 10-second recording will produce approximately 2500 data rows and result in a file size of approximately 167 KB. Use this file size (~167 KB or larger) as a heuristic to verify the 10-second duration if the GUI timer is not clearly visible.
